title: PES - Pandemic Emergency Scenario

funding_references.funder_name: UK Ministry of Defence (MOD) via funding from US/UK DoD BARI

funding_references.award_title: US/UK DoD Bilateral Academic Research Initiative (BARI)

description: The Pandemic Emergency Scenario (PES) dataset comprises neurophysiological and behavioral data from 9 participants engaged in a complex resource allocation task during a simulated pandemic emergency. Participants made sequential decisions about distributing limited medical resources across affected cities while paired with an artificial agent, with EEG recordings capturing neural activity during decision-making and confidence reporting. The dataset includes 64 experimental sequences per participant with synchronized behavioral markers and subjective confidence ratings.
methods_description: EEG data were acquired using a Biosemi ActiveTwo system with 64 electrodes positioned according to the International 10-20 System, supplemented by 8 external channels (2 ECG, 4 EOG). Sampling rate was 2048 Hz. Participants sat approximately 1 meter from an LCD monitor and performed resource allocation decisions via mouse input over a 10-second response window, followed by a 5-second confidence rating phase using the mouse wheel.
